summary: Stated intent: 'make the dashboard feel faster.' Multiple plausible reads (initial paint vs TTI vs perceived responsiveness), unspecified dashboard (admin, customer, internal), and the word 'feel' signals UX perception rather than a raw metric. Ambiguity is high enough to stop and clarify before scoring the rest of the plan.

reversibility.why: Depends on whether the intervention is a client render tweak or a caching/architecture change.

blast_radius.why: Scope of 'dashboard' is unresolved — admin vs customer have very different reach.

compliance_scope.why: Perception tuning does not create a regulated surface on its own.

user_facing_impact.why: Any fix is by definition something users should 'feel'.

novelty.why: Perceived-performance work blends engineering and UX; novelty depends on prior interventions.

scope_effort.why: Could range from skeleton loaders to a caching tier — effort is a multiple of the chosen intervention.

state_complexity.why: Caching or data-shape changes would introduce state; a pure render tweak would not.

operational_risk.why: Depends on whether a caching or streaming change lands in production.

coordination_cost.why: Requires UX + PM + engineering alignment once scope resolves.

specialists.name: requirements-analyst
specialists.why: Owns the clarification and produces the REQ- IDs once scope resolves.

specialists.name: ux-designer
specialists.why: 'Feel faster' is a UX concept; perception work needs a UX owner.

specialists.name: product-manager
specialists.why: Adjudicates scope (which dashboard, which users) and rollout appetite.

phases.name: clarify
phases.why: Only phase emitted confidently — resolve ambiguity before sizing the rest of the chain.

rigor_why: Floor: can only be scored as standard until clarification; may escalate to full if scope crosses compliance or production caching.
complexity_why: Rough mid-range estimate with wide tolerance; true complexity depends on the chosen intervention.
open_questions: By 'faster', do you mean initial paint, time-to-interactive, or perceived responsiveness after an interaction?
Is there a specific user complaint, a metric regressing, or a visible slowdown you want to address?
What's the rollout appetite — feature-flag experiment, or ship to everyone?
recommended_next_steps: /wicked-garden:deliberate — challenge assumptions and reframe the ask
/wicked-garden:jam:quick — surface alternative reads of 'feel faster' across personas

tasks.title: Clarify dashboard scope, 'faster' definition, and rollout appetite
